title: Generating a systemd file for a pod will put "--exit-policy" option after pod name, causing the service to fail
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Running `podman generate systemd --new --name` against a pod without a manually defined `--exit-policy` will cause the option to be appended after the name, which causes the pod creation to fail.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create pod without specifying `--exit-policy`

2. Generate systemd unit with `podman generate systemd --new --name mypod`

**Describe the results you received:**

The following line in the unit file:

```
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/podman pod create --infra-conmon-pidfile %t/pod-dns.pid --pod-id-file %t/pod-dns.pod-id --publish 53:53/tcp --publish 53:53/udp --publish 3000:3000/tcp --publish 853:853/tcp --publish 784:784/udp --publish 853:853/udp --publish 8853:8853/udp --publish 5443:5443/tcp --publish 5443:5443/udp dns --exit-policy=stop
```



**Describe the results you expected:**

The extra option is appended before the pod name.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This unit file can successfully start after moving "dns" to the end of the line. Manually running the command with minor modifications produces the following:

```
# /usr/bin/podman pod create --infra-conmon-pidfile /run/pod-dns.pid --pod-id-file /run/pod-dns.pod-id --publish 53:53/tcp --publish 53:53/udp --publish 3000:3000/tcp --publish 853:853/tcp --publish 784:784/udp --publish 853:853/udp --publish 8853:8853/udp --publish 5443:5443/tcp --publish 5443:5443/udp dns --exit-policy=stop
Error: accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 2
```

Manually specifying `--exit-policy` on the pod before generating the unit file avoids this issue.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.2.0
```

**Output of `podman info`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

A Raspberry Pi 2 device running OpenSUSE Micro OS

comments.body:
I tried it with a fresh pod as well, here are the complete results:
```
> podman pod create no-exit-policy
> podman generate systemd --name --new no-exit-policy
# pod-no-exit-policy.service
# autogenerated by Podman 4.2.0
# Thu Sep  1 12:07:30 UTC 2022

[Unit]
Description=Podman pod-no-exit-policy.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=
Requires=
Before=

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=/bin/rm -f %t/pod-no-exit-policy.pid %t/pod-no-exit-policy.pod-id
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/podman pod create --infra-conmon-pidfile %t/pod-no-exit-policy.pid --pod-id-file %t/pod-no-exit-policy.pod-id no-exit-policy --exit-policy=stop
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman pod start --pod-id-file %t/pod-no-exit-policy.pod-id
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman pod stop --ignore --pod-id-file %t/pod-no-exit-policy.pod-id -t 10
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman pod rm --ignore -f --pod-id-file %t/pod-no-exit-policy.pod-id
PIDFile=%t/pod-no-exit-policy.pid
Type=forking

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

> podman pod create --exit-policy=stop yes-exit-policy
> podman generate systemd --name --new yes-exit-policy
# pod-yes-exit-policy.service
# autogenerated by Podman 4.2.0
# Thu Sep  1 12:08:21 UTC 2022

[Unit]
Description=Podman pod-yes-exit-policy.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=
Requires=
Before=

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=/bin/rm -f %t/pod-yes-exit-policy.pid %t/pod-yes-exit-policy.pod-id
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/podman pod create --infra-conmon-pidfile %t/pod-yes-exit-policy.pid --pod-id-file %t/pod-yes-exit-policy.pod-id --exit-policy=stop yes-exit-policy
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman pod start --pod-id-file %t/pod-yes-exit-policy.pod-id
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman pod stop --ignore --pod-id-file %t/pod-yes-exit-policy.pod-id -t 10
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman pod rm --ignore -f --pod-id-file %t/pod-yes-exit-policy.pod-id
PIDFile=%t/pod-yes-exit-policy.pid
Type=forking

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

Thanks for reaching out and for providing reproducers, @unit511!

I think a good to solve it is to prepend the policy instead of appending it, or deal with the name at the end.

Are you interesting in opening a PR to fix the issue?
I don't really have experience with contributing, but this does seem like a good first issue. I'll look into the process and submit the PR as soon as I can.

Wonderful, thanks, @unit511!

You can find the files here: https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/pkg/systemd/generate

Ping me if you have any question.

@vrothberg I've started working on the PR and while editing the the test cases, I've noticed that the issue only occurs when the pod name was initially specified by the last argument, not the `--name` flag (in which case my naive "fix" actually breaks things quite a lot). I think the easiest solution is to convert those cases to `--name` before further processing.

Update: I've implemented a function that makes sure every pod is specified by `--name` before the command is passed to `setPodExitPolicy`. It passes all current tests and seems to work in my original case, so I just need to write some specific test cases and the PR should be ready to submit.
